hero.name: Alexander Kotov
hero.title: Senior QA Automation Engineer / SDET
hero.subtitle: Python
hero.tagline: Building bulletproof software through intelligent test automation. 6+ years turning complex systems into reliable, well-tested products.
hero.photo_note: QA automation with strong product engineering discipline.
hero.photo_fallback: Add your portrait and this block will automatically display it here.

about.heading: About Me
about.p1: I'm a Senior QA Automation Engineer with 6+ years of experience designing and implementing comprehensive test automation strategies for complex distributed systems.
about.p2: I specialize in microservices architecture testing, API automation, and building CI/CD pipelines that catch bugs before they reach production. My work spans REST, gRPC, GraphQL, and asynchronous messaging systems.
about.p3: I don't just write tests — I architect testing ecosystems that scale with your product. From building frameworks from scratch to migrating legacy systems, I deliver measurable improvements in code quality and team velocity.

mentorship.heading: Mentorship
mentorship.subtitle: I support junior specialists in QA and Python on a volunteer basis when I see real commitment and a strong willingness to grow.
mentorship.title: I help people move from fundamentals into real practice without empty promises or hype.
mentorship.description: From time to time, I take on a mentorship role: helping people build strong QA fundamentals, learn Python for automation, structure their practice, and stay grounded between theory, interviews, and real project work.

points.title: Strong foundation
points.description: I explain QA processes, testing mindset, Python basics, and what the path into automation really looks like in practice.

points.title: Practice and review
points.description: I help with exercises, homework, code review, and error analysis so knowledge turns into repeatable hands-on skill.

points.title: Support along the way
points.description: I give direct feedback, help students stay consistent, and turn effort into skills they can confidently show to employers.

stack.heading: Tech Stack
stack.subtitle: Tools and technologies I use to build robust test automation systems.

experience.heading: Experience
experience.subtitle: A track record of building and scaling test automation across international teams.

jobs.description: Microservices testing for a fintech platform
achievements: Integrated gRPC support into the test framework, expanding service coverage by 40%
Automated async workflow testing across RabbitMQ queues, workers, and PostgreSQL
Built comprehensive data validation tests verifying entity relationships and state transitions
Implemented Allure reporting with detailed logs and attachments, reducing debugging time by 50%
Maintained Docker-based test environments ensuring consistent execution across local and CI/CD pipelines

jobs.description: Full-stack test automation for a global eSIM platform
achievements: Architected complete test automation system covering unit, API, and E2E tests for Python and Elixir backends
Led migration of E2E test suite from Selenium to Playwright during backend rewrite, achieving zero test debt
Built API response comparison tool validating data consistency between legacy and new backends
Developed 5 Directus CMS applications including AI-powered translation (OpenAI, DeepL, Microsoft)
Designed CI/CD pipelines from scratch in GitHub Actions for UI and API test automation
Created Datadog monitoring integration with Slack alerts, reducing incident response time by 70%
Built automated task creation in Linear for failed tests, improving team accountability

jobs.description: Built test framework from scratch for data conversion service
achievements: Designed and built a Python test framework from zero for a data conversion service (PDF, PNG, etc.)
Implemented API testing with Pydantic validation ensuring strict data contract compliance
Created scalable test architecture using Pytest with comprehensive Allure reporting
Containerized test environment with Docker, enabling consistent CI/CD execution

achievements.heading: Key Achievements
achievements.subtitle: Measurable impact across every team I've worked with.

items.title: Faster Debugging
items.description: Implemented Allure reporting with detailed logs at BetaTransfer, reducing diagnosis and failure analysis time by 50%

items.title: Faster Incident Response
items.description: Built Datadog + Slack monitoring at Truely.com, cutting response time from hours to minutes

items.title: Expanded Test Coverage
items.description: Integrated gRPC protocol support at BetaTransfer, covering previously untestable service interactions

items.title: Frameworks and Systems
items.description: Designed and evolved multiple test frameworks and automation systems across teams, from initial setup to stable CI/CD usage

items.title: CMS Apps Delivered
items.description: Built 5 Directus CMS applications at Truely.com, including AI translation integrations

items.title: Test Debt After Migration
items.description: Led Selenium → Playwright migration at Truely.com with zero test coverage loss

items.title: Migrating E2E Tests During Backend Rewrite
items.problem: The company was migrating from Python to Elixir (Phoenix). All existing Selenium E2E tests needed to be rewritten for the new stack without losing coverage.
items.solution: Led the migration to Playwright, built API comparison tools to validate data consistency between backends, and designed a phased rollout strategy.
items.result: Zero test debt after migration. Full E2E and API coverage for the new Elixir backend. Smooth transition with no regression escapes to production.

items.title: Building Test Infrastructure from Zero
items.problem: A data conversion service had no automated testing. Manual testing was slow, unreliable, and couldn't keep up with release cycles.
items.solution: Designed and built a complete Python test framework using Pytest, Requests, and Pydantic. Containerized with Docker and integrated into GitHub Actions CI/CD.
items.result: Fully automated API testing pipeline. Pydantic validation caught data contract violations early. Reduced release cycle from days to hours.

items.title: Async Microservice Testing at Scale
items.problem: Complex async workflows with RabbitMQ queues and multiple microservices made it impossible to verify end-to-end data flow manually.
items.solution: Built automated tests that trace data through message queues, workers, and PostgreSQL. Added gRPC support to the test framework for inter-service validation.
items.result: 40% increase in test coverage. Async bugs caught before deployment. Allure reports with full traceability reduced debugging time by 50%.

items.title: 3D Printing and Modeling
items.description: I design and model parts, then turn them into finished objects through 3D printing. I enjoy taking an idea all the way into a tangible working result.

items.title: Games
items.description: I enjoy games that reward tactics, fast reactions, and resilience: DayZ, Rust, Dota 2, and Diablo. For me, they are another way to sharpen systems thinking and decision-making.

items.title: Sport
items.description: Pilates, pull-up bar training, and hiking help me stay clear-headed and physically grounded. That routine supports discipline, endurance, and recovery.

contact.heading: Let's Work Together
contact.subtitle: I'm open to remote opportunities worldwide. Let's discuss how I can help your team ship better software, faster.
